sections.summary: PA clarifies policy on cloud computing and offshoring data for banks, defining cloud computing as shared pool of configurable computing resources.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines cloud computing for DTIA purposes; specifies PA's regulatory stance on cloud use and data offshoring.

requirements.text: Cloud computing is defined as a model for enabling convenient, on-demand network access to a shared pool of configurable computing resources (e.g. networks, servers, storage facilities, applications and services) that can be rapidly provisioned and released with minimal management effort or service provider interaction.

sections.source_quote: 1.3.1 Cloud computing is defined as a model for enabling convenient, on-demand network access to a shared pool of configurable computing resources (e.g. networks, servers, storage facilities, applications and services) that can be rapidly provisioned and released with minimal management effort or service provider interaction.

sections.summary: PA requires banks to follow risk-based approach for cloud computing and data offshoring, with specific compliance obligations.
sections.dtia_summary: Banks must have data strategy, governance framework, risk assessment, and compliance with local/international laws for cloud/offshoring activities.

requirements.text: Banks must have in place a formally defined and board approved data strategy and data governance framework.
Banks that use cloud computing and/or offshore their data must have a clearly defined policy for such activities, which is aligned with its business strategy and linked to its risk appetite.
Oversight of cloud computing and/or the offshoring of data must be incorporated into the governance structures, processes and procedures within the bank.

Banks must ensure that their risk and control frameworks, including the application thereof, are designed and operating effectively in order to manage the risks associated with the use of cloud computing and/or the offshoring of data.
Prior to undertaking a particular cloud computing and/or data offshoring initiative, the bank must assess whether the risk involved is within its risk appetite.
All strategic investments by the bank, including the use of cloud computing and/or the offshoring of data, must be subjected to appropriate due diligence.
Banks must take all reasonable measures to ensure the confidentiality, integrity and availability of its data, information technology (IT) applications or systems.

Banks that use cloud computing and/or offshore their data must ensure that they remain compliant with applicable legislation and regulations, both locally as well as in any country where the cloud services and/or data are hosted.

The use of cloud computing and/or the offshoring of data must not in any way infringe on the bank's supervisors or prevent any regulatory mandated access to information, nor must it impact on its regulators' ability to fulfil their duties.

Banks must have contingency plans to continue with operations and meet their core obligations, such as regulatory, statutory or otherwise, despite any cloud computing and/or offshoring of data arrangements which may be in place.

Banks must ensure that their intellectual property rights and contractual rights to data are not compromised, despite any cloud computing and/or offshoring of data arrangements which may be in place. Data must always be in a usable, readable and portable state, even when the contract is terminated.
Any cloud computing and/or offshoring of data arrangements must not impact on banks' ability to conduct forensic audits or investigations.
All cloud computing and/or offshoring of data arrangements must be contained in a documented, legally binding agreement.

sections.source_quote: Banks must have in place a formally defined and board approved data strategy and data governance framework.

sections.summary: Banks must provide PA with copies of cloud/offshoring arrangements to auditors for compliance verification.
sections.dtia_summary: Banks must submit documentation to PA for cloud/offshoring arrangements to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.

requirements.text: Kindly ensure that a copy of this directive is made available to your institution's independent auditors.

sections.source_quote: 3.1 Kindly ensure that a copy of this directive is made available to your institution's independent auditors.
